title: Effect Boundary Provenance Enforcement Tasks

implementationImprovementAuditCounter.rule: Each parent task records ownership/call-graph, Effect/implementation-quality, and verification/evidence passes. Add more passes while any finding remains.

principles: Canonical Effect Schema codecs own both Type and Encoded representations.
Unknown and encoded primitives are confined to exact adapters; services accept decoded owner types.
Use Effect-native Schema, Config, HttpClient, Match, Service, Layer, and tagged errors before plain TypeScript boundary helpers.
Keep primary operations flat and linear; expected error handling belongs in the outer pipe.
Do not add unsafe casts, sync production codecs, local DTO mirrors, manual readers/mappers, or helper/common/utils sprawl.
Preserve wire values, persistent bytes, environment names, provider behavior, and framework dispatch.
Lint configuration, architecture docs, AGENTS.md, prd-writer, prd-implementer, and effect-client-wrapper must teach the same boundary provenance policy.

browserVerification.reason: The SPEC changes no visible route or React surface. Direct HTTP contract proof is required for changed webhook and proxy routes.

tasks.title: Establish the boundary provenance audit and exact exception registry
tasks.description: Add one TypeScript compiler-API audit, source fixtures, exact current exception entries, root commands, and architecture rules without changing runtime behavior.
implementationNotes: Inspect public/exported signatures and encode/decode argument types with the TypeScript type checker; do not use a regex-only implementation.

Fail on raw public semantic primitives, inline exported string Schemas, anonymous semantic Config primitives, sync production codecs, direct JSON APIs, unsafe assertions, unregistered raw text/fetch boundaries, and outbound values that bypass canonical encoding.
Represent exceptions by exact file and symbol with owner, boundary kind, canonical codec/service, admitted syntax, and concrete third-party reason.
Fail stale exceptions. Do not use line numbers, counts, broad globs, or permanent debt baselines.
Keep the audit purpose-built in root tooling; do not introduce a generic lint, DTO, Schema helper, or common package.
mandatoryVerification: Positive and negative source fixtures cover every audit rule and stale exception behavior.
The initial report classifies every finding and admits only exact adapter constraints.
Primary Effectful tooling operations are flat meaningful Effect programs with typed errors handled in the outer pipe.
No unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, manual readers/mappers, trivial wrappers, or helper sprawl are introduced.
Focused tooling tests, check-types, Ultracite, Knip, and root verification pass.
Record three parent audit passes with concrete evidence in the active plan.
completionCriteria: A new unregistered raw public boundary fails with file, line, rule, owner guidance, and remediation guidance.
All current exceptions are exact, justified, and mechanically checked for staleness.
Runtime output and provider state are unchanged.

evidence: Ownership/call graph: root tooling owns the compiler-API audit and exact registry without importing application runtime modules.
Implementation quality: reviewed the audit and registry for exact file/symbol/rule matching, stale-entry failure, no generic lint framework, and no runtime behavior changes.

Verification: 22 positive and negative source fixture tests, tooling TypeScript check, check:boundaries, Ultracite, Knip, all seven workspace typechecks, 223 existing package/app tests, and root verification passed with the ignored Preview environment loaded.

tasks.title: Migrate Codex boundaries end to end
tasks.description: Apply provenance enforcement across Codex OAuth, profiles, persistence, filesystem, provider HTTP, SSE, proxy authorization, and operator scripts while preserving exact behavior.
implementationNotes: Use existing Codex owner Schemas for profile IDs, revisions, redirect URIs, paths, tokens, accounts, model IDs, requests, events, and responses; add a Schema only for a real missing owner.
Use decodeUnknownEffect only at host/provider unknown boundaries, decodeEffect for typed encoded data, and encodeEffect for outbound and persisted data.
Keep SSE fragments parser-local, decode completed events before Match, and preserve frames byte for byte.
Move Effectful provider/proof HTTP calls to Effect HttpClient, remove sync codecs from src and scripts, and flatten the profile-cipher nested generator.
Remove audit exceptions as each raw annotation is eliminated; retain only proven third-party constraints.
mandatoryVerification: Codex service APIs expose only canonical Schema-derived decoded types and typed tagged errors.
Profile storage round trips, encrypted values, physical keys, revisions, TTL/locks, OAuth payloads, authorization headers, requests, and SSE frames remain compatible.
Production and operator flows contain no sync Schema calls, global fetch in Effect programs, unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual readers/mappers, or helper sprawl.
Primary operations remain flat Effect.gen/Effect.fn programs and typed errors are handled in the outer pipe.
Focused Codex OAuth, persistence, proxy HTTP, proof-script, typecheck, build, and static audit gates pass.
Record three parent audit passes with concrete evidence in the active plan.
completionCriteria: Codex unknown and encoded values are confined to named adapters from login/import through hosted model streaming.
No Codex-specific raw primitive remains unexplained by an exact third-party constraint.
No production credential, provider account, deployment, or namespace is mutated.

tasks.title: Migrate channel, proxy, config, and persistence boundaries end to end
tasks.description: Apply the same provenance model to Eve, the agent channel, Sendblue, the proxy app, runtime config, and shared persistence adapters without changing routing or wire behavior.
implementationNotes: Confine Eve callback unknown/Promise primitives to the framework adapter and keep Eve's typed event map as dispatch authority.
Use app/channel-owned Schemas for config, preflight, inbound/outbound content, coordinates, replay values, and safe diagnostics.
Use Config.schema for semantic configuration and preserve Redacted secrets without unwrap/redecode cycles.
Keep native KeyValueStore and Upstash primitives private; decode SDK unknown results and encode all domain records through their owner codecs.
Use Effect HttpClient in proxy proof/smoke programs and canonical response/SSE codecs in handlers.
mandatoryVerification: Signed webhook verification, sender mapping, session routing, replay claims, outbound delivery, and message.completed behavior remain unchanged.
Proxy authorization, status codes, content types, error responses, streaming output, and Vercel fetch composition pass direct HTTP tests.
Persistent replay/claim keys, values, prefixes, TTLs, and atomic transaction behavior are byte-compatible.
All changed APIs use owner Schema-derived types, Match for material decoded unions, flat Effect control flow, and typed errors in the outer pipe.
No unsafe casts, sync production codecs, local DTO mirrors, manual readers/mappers, trivial wrappers, or helper sprawl remain.
Record three parent audit passes with concrete evidence in the active plan.
completionCriteria: All agent, channel, proxy, config, and persistence raw values are decoded/encoded at exact adapters or registered as unavoidable constraints.
Focused app/package tests, direct HTTP checks, builds, typechecks, and the boundary audit pass.
No frontend, webhook, provider, credential, deployment, or namespace state is changed.

tasks.title: Enable hard gates and reconcile documentation and skills
tasks.description: Enable the selected Effect language-service diagnostics, place boundary and setup checks in root verification, remove resolved exceptions, and reconcile lint configuration, architecture/package/app documentation, AGENTS.md, and the relevant skills.
implementationNotes: Configure diagnosticSeverity in tsconfig.base.json for the approved provenance and Effect-flow rules; do not enable deterministicKeys in this migration.
Make CI prove that TypeScript is patched with the Effect language service before relying on diagnostics.
Reduce the exception registry to unavoidable third-party/framework constraints and fail stale entries.

Update Effect patterns with Type/Encoded, codec, adapter-exception, Config, HTTP, persistence, error, Match, and helper-admission rules; update testing/quality with the executable commands, fixtures, compatibility proof, and stale-pattern scans.
Update repo structure and AGENTS.md with concise ownership and boundary defaults; CLAUDE.md remains the symlinked view and is not edited independently.
Update prd-writer so boundary-crossing specs must name canonical codecs, decoded/encoded sides, adapters, exception needs, and inbound/outbound call graphs.

Update prd-implementer so its mandatory prompt, review bar, and audit loop reject raw public primitives, unsafe construction, unencoded outward writes, stale exceptions, and missing boundary-audit evidence.

Rewrite effect-client-wrapper examples around named operations, Config.schema, request encoding, provider-result decoding, Schema tagged errors, and live/mock Layers; remove generic public use callbacks, raw id strings, primitive semantic config, instanceof branching, and unchecked SDK result escape.
Update package READMEs and app runbooks only where public ownership or verification changed.
Record final production/test/CLI call graphs and exact verification evidence in the active plan.
mandatoryVerification: The boundary audit reports zero unexplained findings across production source and operator scripts.
Effect diagnostics report zero errors with the selected rules configured as errors.
Final changed operations retain flat meaningful Effect.gen/Effect.fn control flow with typed errors handled in the outer pipe.
No broad suppression, ignore expansion, numeric debt baseline, unsafe cast, DTO mirror, manual mapper/reader, or helper sprawl was used.
Stale-pattern scans prove AGENTS.md, Effect architecture docs, prd-writer, prd-implementer, and effect-client-wrapper contain no contradictory boundary guidance or obsolete code examples.
Root formatting/check, Knip, all-workspace typecheck, tests, builds, task JSON parse, diff check, and bun run verification pass.
Docs, source contracts, exception registry, call graphs, and verification commands agree.
Record three parent audit passes with concrete evidence in the active plan.
completionCriteria: CI prevents new raw or unencoded boundary data from entering the repository.
Every remaining primitive exception is exact, external-interface-driven, and enclosed by a canonical codec/service.
Repository instructions and skills cause future specs, implementation prompts, reviews, and provider wrappers to apply the same enforced provenance model.
The SPEC and task ledger are marked implemented only after all evidence is recorded.
